summary: Hotz delivers a scathing attack on people who entered the technology industry purely for money and status rather than genuine passion for building things. He accuses these 'entryists' of corrupting tech culture and calls for radical action: making everything free to drive them out. The post argues that the profit motive has attracted the wrong people to technology. His proposed solution is to eliminate the financial incentive entirely, leaving only those who truly care about the work.
key_points: Technology has been invaded by people motivated solely by money and status, not genuine interest
These 'entryists' are harmful to tech culture and need to be expelled
They won't leave voluntarily because they're still profiting
The solution is to make technology free, removing the financial incentive that attracted them
money_quotes: You heard there was money in tech. You heard there was status in tech. You showed up.
You never cared about technology. You cared about enriching yourself.
You are an entryist piece of shit. And it's time for you to leave.
Give it all away to everyone for free. Then you'll have no reason to be here.
key_insight: The only way to purify technology culture is to eliminate profit entirely, because mercenaries only leave when there's nothing left to take.
